mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate signals tighter monetary policy, raising discount rates and corporate borrowing costs, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations following a month-over-month increase in FEDFUNDS, or the Rank IC exceeds -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that precedes corporate revenue downgrades, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the signal's t-statistic falls below 1.5 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and an acceleration in consumer prices captures stagflationary demand compression, where falling real consumer expenditures and rising input costs jointly erode corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and CPIAUCSL adds less than 1% incremental explanatory power over main effects in a walk-forward regression, or negative returns occur in fewer than 50% of joint-signal instances.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production coincident with an elevated federal funds rate signals that restrictive monetary policy is transmitting into real supply destruction, amplifying downside earnings revisions and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations during periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4% and INDPRO is declining month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales signals stronger consumer demand and immediate revenue upside for consumer-facing sectors, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances following a month-over-month increase in RSAFS, or the Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in the federal funds rate and a deceleration in consumer prices signals a restrictive monetary policy overshoot that tightens financial conditions disproportionately, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of rising FEDFUNDS and falling CPIAUCSL fails to generate negative returns in more than 50% of joint-signal instances, or the interaction term's t-statistic is below 1.5 in a walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 signals a tight labor market and robust income growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or the Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 50,000 units signals a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction and durable goods, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the Rank IC is above -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
